story.iWant: functions to throw standard PowerShell exceptions on failures
story.soThat: calling scripts can implement their own error recovery
story.tasks: - Define exception hierarchy (AC: #1, #2, #3) - Create LogManagerException base class - Create CompressionException for compression failures - Create TokenResolutionException for path token errors - Ensure all exceptions extend LogManagerException - Implement error handling pattern in cmdlets (AC: #2, #3, #4) - Use ThrowTerminatingError for all failure scenarios - Include error ID, category, and target object - Write actionable error messages with remediation guidance - Create error handling tests (AC: #5) - Test path not found throws ItemNotFoundException - Test permission denied throws UnauthorizedAccessException - Test invalid parameters throw ArgumentException - Test compression failure throws CompressionException - Verify no silent failures occur

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. All functions use standard PowerShell error handling 2. Throw terminating errors for failures (no silent failures) 3. Error messages are clear and actionable 4. No built-in retry logic (caller's responsibility) 5. Unit tests validate error conditions trigger exceptions

doc.title: Technical Specification
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts - Exception Hierarchy

docs.doc: Exception Hierarchy: - LogManagerException (base): Inherits from System.Exception - CompressionException: Inherits from LogManagerException (compression failures) - TokenResolutionException: Inherits from LogManagerException (invalid token resolution) All exceptions should provide constructors for: message only, message + inner exception

Error scenarios: - Path not found → ItemNotFoundException - Permission denied → UnauthorizedAccessException with operation context - Compression fails (7-Zip exit non-zero) → CompressionException with stderr output - S3 connection fails → TransferException wrapping AWS error All exceptions via ThrowTerminatingError() with ErrorId, Category, TargetObject
FR012: Fail-Fast Error Handling - All cmdlets throw terminating errors on failures. No automatic retry logic. Clear, actionable error messages. Exception hierarchy enables targeted catch blocks.

Foundation layer framework. Cross-cutting concern for all functions. MVP discipline: No complex error recovery or retry logic. All cmdlets must fail fast with clear error messages. Use PowerShell standard error categories.

artifact.reason: Example cmdlet showing parameter validation and potential error handling patterns
Example of ArgumentException throwing for parameter validation

constraints.constraint: Foundation layer framework - affects all cmdlets and services
All exceptions must inherit from LogManagerException base class
Provide constructors: (string message) and (string message, Exception inner)
Cmdlets must use ThrowTerminatingError() for all failures (not throw directly)
Include ErrorRecord with: ErrorId, ErrorCategory, TargetObject
Error messages must be actionable with remediation guidance
NO retry logic - fail fast and let caller handle recovery
NO silent failures - every error condition must throw
Use standard .NET/PowerShell exceptions where appropriate (ArgumentException, ItemNotFoundException, UnauthorizedAccessException)
Provide XML documentation for all exception classes

tests.standards: Unit tests: xUnit for exception throwing validation. Integration tests: Pester for cmdlet error handling behavior. Mock file system and external dependencies to trigger error conditions. Test naming: MethodName_ErrorCondition_ThrowsExpectedException

ideas.test: Create LogManagerException base class with message and inner exception constructors
Test path not found throws ItemNotFoundException
Test invalid olderThan < youngerThan throws ArgumentException
Test compression failure (7-Zip non-zero exit) throws CompressionException
Test missing token context (e.g., {server} but ServerName null) throws TokenResolutionException
Verify ThrowTerminatingError is used in cmdlets (not direct throw)
Verify error messages include specific failure context (file path, operation attempted)
Verify error messages include remediation guidance where appropriate
Verify NO retry logic exists in any cmdlet or service
Test all error paths to ensure no silent failures (comprehensive negative testing)
Test ErrorRecord includes correct ErrorCategory (InvalidArgument, ResourceUnavailable, etc.)
